name: Balneari de Cardó - Circular by the Roca Foradada
name_original: Balneari de Cardó - Circular por la Roca Foradada
region_intro: Hiking around Benifallet offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the Ebro River and surrounding mountains that reach altitudes of up to 600 meters. The region features varied topography, including the dramatic Serra de Cardó mountain range and picturesque riverside scenery. This area provides a rich environment for exploration, with well-maintained trails catering to different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Benifallet The most popular hiking route is Balneari de Cardó - Circular by the Roca Foradada, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cardó Spa – Creu de Santos Summit loop from Salt del Frare, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail leads to a summit, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Cardó Spa – Arc de Cardó loop from Salt del Frare, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through the Cardó Spa area, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Hiking around Benifallet is defined by the Ebro River, the Serra de Cardó mountain range, and varied mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to more challenging treks. The routes in Benifallet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Almost 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Benifallet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Benifallet?
A: Benifallet offers a diverse range of landscapes, from the majestic Ebro River and its picturesque riverside scenery to the dramatic mountainous terrain of the Serra de Cardó. You'll find routes with moderate ascents and more challenging treks, providing varied views of the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Benifallet?
A: While Benifallet features many moderate and difficult routes, the region does offer options suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa – Arc de Cardó loop from Salt del Frare&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate trail that takes about 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benifallet?
A: The most pleasant temperatures for hiking in Benifallet are typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer ideal conditions to enjoy the outdoor pursuits without the extreme heat of summer or the cooler temperatures of winter.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see on a hike?
A: Benifallet is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the unique rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch&lt;/a&gt;, visit the natural springs at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt;, or discover various caves such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3413673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluminosa Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also lead to viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/692380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Santos Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benifallet?
A: Yes, Benifallet has several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951800266/balneari-de-cardo-circular-by-the-roca-foradada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balneari de Cardó - Circular by the Roca Foradada&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.9 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3973418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa – Creu de Santos Summit loop from Salt del Frare&lt;/a&gt;, offering summit views.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Benifallet?
A: The hiking trails in Benifallet cater to a range of abilities. The majority of the 13 routes are classified as moderate, with 10 routes falling into this category. There are also 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, but no easy routes are listed.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Benifallet?
A: The hiking area around Benifallet is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Nearly 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained network of trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, Benifallet offers difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40350699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda – Old railway viaduct loop from Antiga Estació de Benifallet&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.5 km trail that takes over 5 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation gain and varied terrain.

Q: Can I find trails that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: Yes, the region is known for the Greenway de la Val de Zafán, an old railway line transformed into a scenic nature trail. This provides an accessible and enjoyable route for both hikers and cyclists, offering a different perspective of the landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Cardó Spa area?
A: The Cardó Spa area is a popular starting point for several hiking loops. You can choose from routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3973418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa – Creu de Santos Summit loop from Salt del Frare&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa – Hermitage of Sant Àngel Ruins loop from Salt del Frare&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varying distances and elevation gains within this picturesque area.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on Benifallet's trails?
A: Benifallet's mountainous terrain means you can expect significant elevation changes on many trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951800266/balneari-de-cardo-circular-by-the-roca-foradada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balneari de Cardó - Circular by the Roca Foradada&lt;/a&gt; involves over 640 meters of ascent, while even shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardó Spa – Arc de Cardó loop&lt;/a&gt; still feature over 220 meters of climbing.
tips:
It is a small, rectangular, single-storey hermitage, made of lime and mortar and with a gabled roof without a gable.
The primary purpose of the chapel of Sant Roc was to provide a secluded place for the characters of the Reformed Carmelite Order who wanted, for a few days, to lead the life of an anchorite, but in a more comfortable place than the one that characterized the first hermitages built during the first half of the 17th century in the Vall de Cardó, following the founding path of Teresa de Jesús. Although the date of consecration is unknown, it is known that it was the last to be built. It was abandoned, like the rest, in 1835 due to the confiscation of Mendizábal.
Its waters, known since ancient times as Ronya due to its healing properties, allowed the hermitage to be transformed, from the beginning of the use of the valley as a spa, into the pavilion for inhalations, sprays and partial bathrooms. But as it was very far from the main buildings, at the beginning of the 19th century the waters of the Ronya or Sant Roc were led to other sources that were closer After the Civil War, the hermitage of Sant Elies or del Borboll concentrated the services hydro-therapeutics, and that of Sant Roc was abandoned.

It is a natural bridge of good dimensions that is closer to an arch than to a holed rock of the many, which is why it is also known as the Arch of Cardó.
Currently the path that goes from Portell de Cosp to the crossroads with the paths of Sant Roc, all the way through the Cova Lluminosa, is quite clean but you have to take into account that it is dirt and long pants are very suitable.

Curious rock formation reminiscent of Thor's Hammer or maybe it's just a cinephile influence.
In any case, they are worth seeing and the views over the Vall de Cardó and the Spa are brutal.
The path that goes up from the path of Sant Roc in the direction of Portell de Cardó, was quite lost, much better the one that continues until it connects again with this one.
Source under old yew, wonderful as a resting place

Hermitage destroyed since ancient times. Since the other thirteen are quite similar, we assume a rectangular plan of reduced dimensions with irregular bricks bound with mortar.
As Beguer points out in his book "El valle de Cardó", the hermitage of Sant Bernat was the first to be built and it seems that it was also the first to collapse. Shortly after its destruction, it suffered the consequences of one of the several fires that occurred in this area.

Legend has it that a hermit from Cardó was caught in the company of a local woman, enjoying the meat in the sacred precincts of the hermitage of Sant Bernat. As a result of this fact, based on the consideration that the place was no longer sacred, the hermitage was set on fire and the place remained abandoned. So abandoned that today very few people had heard of it and no one knew of its whereabouts and no one is known to have visited it for decades. Curiosity, knowledge of the terrain and personal effort have made this beautiful route a reality and allow us to get to know places as special as the sweet oak forest, the Pic de Calces or the venerable old path of Benifallet.
